what_it_does: Converts JSONL conversation logs from Claude Code or Codex rollouts into human-readable Markdown or styled HTML transcripts, preserving metadata, messages, tool use/results, sidechain markers, and token usage.
observations: Supports two distinct input formats: Claude Code ConversationMessage JSONL and Codex rollout JSONL.
Markdown output supports an optional 1-indexed line range slice.
HTML output embeds a complete inline stylesheet and uses anchor links per message.
Tool results are paired with their tool_use blocks by scanning the next five user messages.
Assistant messages with no content are kept if they contain token usage.
Raw HTML in assistant markdown is sanitized via a custom marked renderer.
Sidechain messages are visually grouped with start/end markers in Markdown and a wrapper div in HTML.
Codex metadata is extracted from session_meta and turn_context entries.
standouts: Dual-format parser for Claude Code and Codex logs.
Heuristic tool-result pairing and sidechain grouping.
conventions: TypeScript with exported top-level functions and private helpers.
Uses the marked library for Markdown parsing.
Defensive JSON parsing with try/catch fallbacks.
1-indexed, inclusive line range for Markdown slicing.
camelCase naming and inline styling for HTML output.
concerns: Significant duplication of filtering, message handling, and tool pairing logic between Markdown and HTML paths.
Heavy use of any for Codex payloads and tool blocks weakens type safety.
Tool-result search is capped at five messages and may miss delayed or out-of-order results.
isMarkdown uses an arbitrary two-pattern threshold that may misclassify plain text.
JSONL parse errors are silently skipped in isCodexRollout and could mask malformed input.
notes: A presentation-layer formatter that turns agent conversation JSONL into reviewable Markdown or styled HTML, with dedicated handling for both Claude Code and Codex harness logs.
questions: What downstream system consumes the Markdown versus HTML outputs?
How does toolUseResult relate to the tool_result blocks inside message.content?
Why is the line-range slice only offered for Markdown and not HTML?
What exactly does isSidechain signify semantically?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file is clearly a conversation-log formatter for multiple harness formats.
apparent_themes: Safe rendering of untrusted assistant output.
intention_signals: Make machine-generated conversation logs easy for humans to read, share, and audit while safely rendering assistant markdown and surfacing tool interactions and sidechains.
novelty: Combines Claude Code and Codex rollout formatting in a single module, with sidechain visual grouping and a heuristic tool-result pairing mechanism.
contribution: A self-contained formatter that renders JSONL conversation logs as Markdown or HTML, preserving metadata, tool use/result relationships, sidechain structure, token usage, and sanitizing rendered HTML.
summary_for_embedding: TypeScript module that formats JSONL conversation logs from Claude Code or Codex into Markdown or styled HTML transcripts.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a human-readable, reviewable rendering of agent conversation logs with metadata, tool use, sidechains, and token usage.

symbols.does: Defines the shape of a Claude Code conversation log entry.
symbols.description: Interface describing a conversation message with uuid, parentUuid, timestamp, type, sidechain flag, session metadata, nested message content, token usage, and an optional toolUseResult field.

symbols.does: Parses JSONL and returns a Markdown transcript, optionally sliced by line range.
symbols.description: Exported entry point that detects Codex rollouts and otherwise parses Claude Code JSONL, filters system/empty messages, and renders metadata, messages, tool use/results, sidechains, and token usage as Markdown.

symbols.description: Helper that extracts text from output or falls back to pretty-printed JSON, used when rendering Codex tool results.
